feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Comunidade SIT Brazil
entry.content:
Este blog tratrá atualizações do que está ocorrendo pela Comunidade SAP no Brasil e seus grupos locais!

https://sitbrazil.com/

entry.title: Página Web da Comunidade SAP do Brasil está no ar pela 1ª vez!
entry.content:
Agora é possível ver tudo que está acontecendo na Comunidade SAP no Brasil em 1 só lugar com um visual desenhado em alto nível.

Visitem: https://sitbrazil.com/

e não esqueçam de dizer o que acharam!

entry.title: A comunidade SAP no Brasil tem seu primeiro site no ar
entry.content:
A comunidade SAP no Brasil acaba de estrear seu primeiro site. Agora, você poderá saber todas as novidadades da comunidade em um só lugar. 

O site está incrível! Visitem: https://sitbrazil.com/  e não esqueçam de dizer o que acharam.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, January 30th, 2025
entry.content:
Description

What’s New in SAP Build: Q4 2024 Webinar

  • Webinar Recordings (for Public) https://workzone.one.int.sap/site#workzone-home&/groups/zJfVxkPC5b2kQfs6YahqyX/content?folder_id=TfxCd7kH9hdfZ1hdvxFyBU&view_mode=list
  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-blog-posts/what-s-new-in-sap-build-q4-2024-edition/ba-p/13980162

SAP Build Code – What’s New January 2025

  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – January 2025 Edition: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/product-updates-for-sap-build-code-january-2025-edition/ba-p/13993021
  • Deprecation of Java Tools in SAP Web IDE: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/deprecation-of-java-tools-in-sap-web-ide/ba-p/13997766
  • SAP Build Code Joule Evolves: Unveiling Multi-Agent Support for Advanced CAP Application Generation: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-code-joule-evolves-unveiling-multi-agent-support-for-advanced-cap/ba-p/13995395

Upcoming CAP January Release 2025

  • npm @SAP/cds-dk https://www.npmjs.com/package/@sap/cds-dk
  • CAP Release page January 2025 | capire

SAP Community active on Bluesky social media

  • SAP Community Account on Bluesky: https://bsky.app/profile/sapcommunity.bsky.social
  • Starter Pack SAP Community:  https://bsky.app/starter-pack/sapcommunity.bsky.social/3lfnowo7ykb2l
  • Starter Pack SAP Developer Advocates:  https://bsky.app/starter-pack/sapcommunity.bsky.social/3lgbebsq43a2x
  • Starter Pack SAP Champions:  https://bsky.app/starter-pack/sapcommunity.bsky.social/3lgbesf6hqa2r
  • Starter Pack SAP Mentors:  https://bsky.app/starter-pack/sapcommunity.bsky.social/3lgbem6f4u32p

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 What’s New in SAP Build: Q4 2024 Webinar

2:13 SAP Build Code What’s New Jan 2025

5:11 Upcoming CAP January Release 2025

6:14 SAP Community active on Bluesky social media

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro]This is the SAP Developer News for January 30th, 2025.

[Shrini] Hello SAP developers. I hope many of you got a chance to watch the webinar yesterday on the new SAP Build features released in Q4 2024. There were updates on product-specific highlights on tools such as SAP Build Process Automation, build code, build apps, and WorkZone with some cool demos on the new features. Here are some of my favorites. The design time UI in build apps has been redesigned allowing for a more intuitive user experience. Different pages in an app are easily accessible via drop down. Less frequently used tasks are positioned under app settings. Preview and publish options are now more prominent. In the process automation, now you can create and manage process variants. Process variants allow a variant template developer to create processes with pre-configured settings and a variant management user, usually a business configuration expert can modify the template variant to suit his needs which changes like change or order of variance, modify the start condition, release and deploy variants, etc. In WorkZone standard edition, the integration of Joule is a game changer. Joule in WorkZone provides answers and insights from any connected system via prompts, eliminating the need to switch between different tools and user interfaces. Note that this feature is not available in the free trial. Joule integrated in SAP Build Code, now it's seamless migration of SAP UI5 applications from JavaScript to TypeScript. SAP Build Code has an adaptation editor tool which is a new tool for extending SAP UI5 applications. It replaces the SAP UI5 Visual Editor. There are many more features. release in the last quarter, do check out the webinar recording and the blog post explaining all the features, the link to which are in the description below.

[Thomas] My colleague, Shri has already brought you some of the news about SAP build enhancements that were released in January of this year. I want to do a little breakout there on specific enhancements for SAP build code. Of course, build code is part of SAP build build and a little bit. bit of the news was covered in his segment, but for professional developers, I want to give you a little additional information. First of all, in SAP Build Code, we see the new project overview. This is to really replace the project Explorer and give you a better visualization of your project and in particular match up the structure to what you're seeing when you're using the storyboard editor. Next, we see improvements when working with remote function calls. There we've added the ability for the run configuration to be updated automatically as well as support both basic and principle propagation. Next, we see the inline editing for CDS entities. This allows you to edit entities and their attributes without having to switch between the graphical editor and the text or code-based editor. And we see the deprecation of Java tools for SAP WebIDE Now, this is an important note because this has to do with the deprecation of the SAP Java BuildPack version 1. Now, that deprecation is going to take place in June of 2025, and at that time, the older tools in the SAP Web IDE for Java development will also be deprecated. Now, this just means that you need to be using the SAP Business Application Studio, which we recommend that you use anyway. So by June 2025, please plan on moving your CAP Java and other Java-based, uh, tooling-based projects to the business application studio. And finally, the last item has to do with the Joule code generative, uh, capabilities in SAP build code. We see the agent, uh, are the, Joule assistant going multi-agent with the latest enhancements, particularly we see this in the CAP, new project generation. And now what you can do is you can give it a robust description of what you want in your entire CAP application to do, and in a single prompt, it will respond by both generating the application, generating sample data, and generating application logic for what you described. This used to be several different calls and interactions that you'd have to have with the Joule assistant and now, with it being multi-agent, you can do that in all one prompt. So look for all these enhancements in SAP Build Code. And of course, the blog post are all in the links to the show note of this episode.

[Kevin] The January 2025 cap releases right around the corner and we got some great release notes for you. The new CDS parser replacing ANTLR is almost done. With the new parser, you will get reduced installations and faster parsing, as well as improved code completion. And with the new release, you will also be able to use enum symbols when defining a default value. More enum symbols support will come in the future. And on the Java side, you can now use predicates as select items to evaluate Boolean expressions on the database. Odata v401 allows you to invoke functions using implicit parameter aliases, and this invocation style is now also supported by the CAP Java runtime. And CDS debug has been extended for Java, so you can easily debug local and remote Java applications. And lastly, I want to mention that IntelliJ has now SAP CDS language support. There are even more great news about the upcoming release, and you can read all about them in detail on the CAP release page.

[Josh] Hey there, it's Mr. Bluebeard, and I hope you are enjoying the blue sky. It is very windy and cold. outside today, so I came inside to talk to you about Blue Sky, not that Blue Sky, but about the social media site, Blue Sky. SAP is getting more and more active on Blue Sky. We've got the SAP community there. They've had a great feature called starter packs. So if you don't want to follow just the SAP community site, but also a bunch of individuals within other groups, you can quickly grab a starter pack for something like the SAP mentors, champions, developer advocates, or you can just continue to follow individuals. I've got an account there along with a bunch of other people that are inside of those other groups. It's just a great place where information can go and live freely. It was started by some people that started at different social media site years ago. And this is just basically a reboot of a place to go for short, micro -blogging, links, pictures, videos to things you care about. So follow accounts like the SAP community or maybe the UI5 group if you're into that. So there are more and more groups popping up every day. And Blue Sky is not just a song by a great band called ELO Right, DJ? It's a great place to go and share information. We'll see you there. Bye.

entry.title: Site do SITBRAZIL com todos os eventos da Comunidade
entry.content:
Foi lançado um Site SITBRAZIL: https://sitbrazil.com/

O grupo de Fortaleza está lá representado. Este site tratrá atualizações do que está ocorrendo pela Comunidade SAP no Brasil e seus grupos locais, bem como vai permitir uma divulgação mais profissional dos eventos, bem como a partilha de informações entre todas as organizações de eventos SAP no Brazil.

entry.title: Speaker Registration Now Open for SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 1st March 2025 – Business AI Edition
entry.content:
Learn more about the event in our announcement blog post.

Speaker Tracks for Your Proposal:

  1. Track 2: Building AI Applications on SAP BTP
    Delve into the technicalities of building AI-powered applications on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). Share how developers and architects can leverage SAP BTP to create impactful AI solutions that drive business value.
  2. Track 3: Business AI Solutions from the Ecosystem (Partners & Customers)
    Highlight successful AI solutions developed by SAP partners and customers. Present case studies, implementation experiences, and collaborative projects that demonstrate the innovative use of Business AI within the SAP ecosystem.
  3. Track 4: Embedded AI Across Solutions (SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, and Other SAP Products)
    Explore how Embedded AI is integrated into various SAP products like SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, and CX. Discuss how these intelligent features are transforming business processes and enhancing user experiences.

Why Speak at SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2025 1st Edition – Business AI Edition?

  • Showcase Your Expertise: Share your knowledge with an engaged audience of SAP professionals.
  • Network with Peers: Connect with fellow experts, partners, and SAP enthusiasts.
  • Gain Recognition: Boost your professional profile and gain visibility within the SAP community.

How to Register:

Ready to share your insights at SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2025 – Business AI Edition?
Register here to become a speaker and contribute to one of the most anticipated SAP community events of the year.

Next Steps:

  1. Proposal Review: Our expert team will review your submission. If shortlisted, we will reach out for more details.
  2. Internal Review: After a thorough review, we will notify you about the next steps, including presentation formats and promotional details.

We look forward to your valuable contributions and hope to see you at SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2025 1st Edition!

Best regards,
Ranjeet

entry.title: Call for Speakers: SAP Inside Track Sydney 2025, March 21
entry.content:
We’re excited to kick off the year with SAP Inside Track Sydney 2025, and speaker submissions are now open! This premier event is a great opportunity for SAP professionals, enthusiasts, and thought leaders to share their insights, innovations, and expertise with a dynamic community. Whether you're a seasoned speaker or a passionate first-timer, we invite you to be part of this first SAP Inside Track event of the year and contribute to an inspiring knowledge exchange!

Event Overview

 
Date: March 21, 2025 (1pm  - 5pm)
Location: Sydney
Venue:  SAP Australia, Level 13/1 Denison St, North Sydney NSW 2060

This community-driven event is all about learning, networking, and sharing insights with fellow SAP professionals, developers, and experts. After many year, we are changing the format to a half-day format to accomodate more topics and speakers from the #SAPCommunity. Expect some great content and discussions across different topics. 

Call for Speakers – We Want YOU!

Got something cool to share about SAP technologies and solutions? Whether you’re an experienced speaker or new to the stage, we’d love to hear from you!

Topics of Interest:

  • Enterprise Architecture 
  • RISE with SAP / GROW with SAP
  • Application Development & Automation
  • Data & Analytics 
  • Business AI & use cases relevant to businesses 

Why Speak at SAP Inside Track?

📌 Share Your Knowledge – Inspire the SAP community with your expertise.
📌 Expand Your Network – Meet and connect with fellow SAP professionals.
📌 Boost Your Profile – Gain recognition within the SAP ecosystem.
📌 Give Back to the Community – Contribute to SAP’s growing knowledge hub

How to Register

Submit your speaker proposal here by filling out the online form. If you have any questions, you can leave a comment below.

What Happens Next?

Our expert team reviews your proposal. If shortlisted, we’ll contact you for more details. Once the final selections are made, we’ll guide you through the next steps—presentation format, social media promotions, and more!  Don’t miss out—join us in making SAP Inside Track Sydney 2025 an unforgettable event!

We look forward to seeing you. Do subscribe to the Sydney Group to receive notifications of similar events.

entry.title: Successful First Online Meeting for SAP Community Vancouver!
entry.content:
We are thrilled to share that our first online meeting for the SAP Community Vancouver went smoothly and was a great success!

We had the pleasure of being joined by four enthusiastic members:

  • Moldir Abdikerim
  • Cherine Cañete Simbajon
  • Prabakaran Ganesan
  • Vellaikamban Balaji


The session kicked off with each of us introducing ourselves and sharing how SAP has become an integral part of our career journeys. It was inspiring to hear everyone’s unique experiences and the diverse ways SAP has impacted our professional lives.

We also took some time to get to know more about the SAP Community and the various resources and opportunities it offers.

The session concluded on a high note with a giveaway of two free e-book vouchers from SAP PRESS, adding an extra layer of excitement to our meeting.

A big thank you to everyone who participated and made this event memorable. We look forward to many more engaging and insightful sessions in the future!

Stay tuned for updates on our next meeting.

#SAPCommunity #Vancouver #Networking #CareerGrowth #SAPPRESS

entry.title: Cline for SAP AI Core: Bringing Cline's Power to the SAP Ecosystem
entry.content:
Introduction

In the ever-evolving landscape of AI-assisted development, integrating advanced AI capabilities directly into your workflow can significantly enhance productivity and innovation. I'm excited to introduce Cline for SAP AI Core, a new Visual Studio Code (VS Code) extension that brings the power of SAP AI Core into the agentic coding experience of Cline. This extension enables SAP developers to seamlessly interact with state-of-the-art AI models through their SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) accounts, unlocking new levels of efficiency in coding and business application development.

Why Cline for SAP AI Core?

The original Cline extension has been transformative for developers, offering an AI partner that plans before coding, ensuring thoughtful and efficient code generation. However, it doesn't integrate external AI providers outside its direct control. Recognizing the need for SAP AI Core support within the SAP development ecosystem, I created this dedicated extension to bridge the gap.

With Cline for SAP AI Core, developers working with SAP BTP can now access advanced AI models natively within their VS Code environment, ensuring a seamless and efficient workflow tailored for enterprise use.

Key Features

  • Seamless Integration with SAP AI Core: Directly connect to AI Core services within VS Code, allowing for a unified development environment.

  • Access to Advanced AI Models: Utilize models such as Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Claude 3 Sonnet, Claude 3 Haiku, Claude 3 Opus, GPT-4o, GPT-4o Mini, and GPT-4, empowering developers with capabilities like code completion, debugging assistance, and natural language understanding.

  • Tailored for SAP BTP Users: Leverage your SAP AI Core infrastructure within Cline for AI-driven development and automation, enhancing productivity and innovation.

How to Get Started

Installation:

  • Open VS Code.

  • Navigate to the Extensions tab (Windows: Ctrl+Shift+X- Mac: Cmd+Shift+X).

  • Search for "Cline for SAP AI Core".

  • Click "Install" and restart VS Code if needed.

Configuration:

  • Open Settings in Cline for SAP AI Core.

  • Select SAP AI Core as your API provider.

  • Enter the required authentication details:

    • AI Core Client ID

    • AI Core Client Secret

    • AI Core Base URL (e.g., https://api.ai.internalprod.eu-central-1.aws.ml.hana.ondemand.com/v2)

    • AI Core Auth URL (e.g., https://aicoreexample.authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token)

    • AI Core Resource Group

  • Select your preferred AI Model from the list of available models.

Unlocking New Possibilities

By integrating Cline with SAP AI Core, developers can:

  • Enhance Code Quality: Leverage AI to generate well-structured code, reducing errors and improving maintainability.

  • Automate Repetitive Tasks: Utilize AI to handle routine coding tasks, allowing developers to focus on more complex aspects of development.

  • Accelerate Development Cycles: With AI assistance, rapidly prototype and develop applications, shortening time-to-market.

For a comprehensive tutorial on how Cline can transform your coding workflow, you might find the following video helpful:

Cline + VS Code Changed How I Code Forever

Additionally, to learn more about how to use Cline in your SAP development workflow, check out this resource:

Unlocking the Power of AI-Assisted ABAP Coding with VS Code and Cline

Join the Future of AI-Assisted Development

Cline for SAP AI Core opens up new possibilities for AI-driven coding and automation within the SAP ecosystem. By seamlessly integrating SAP AI Core into VS Code, developers are empowered with cutting-edge AI models to enhance productivity and innovation.

Give it a try today and experience the future of AI-assisted SAP development!

I would love to hear your feedback and experiences! Let’s discuss in the comments below. 🚀

entry.title: Deploying SAP CAP Services on SAP BTP Using Docker
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog post, I will walk you through how I deployed an SAP CAP (Cloud Application Programming) project on SAP BTP using Docker instead of the traditional Node.js runtime. This approach provides better containerization, scalability, and easier environment management.

Why Use Docker for SAP CAP?

Traditionally, SAP CAP services are deployed directly using Node.js. However, by using Docker, we gain several advantages:

  • Portability: The application runs consistently across different environments.

  • Isolation: Dependencies are encapsulated within the container.

  • Scalability: Easily deploy multiple instances.

  • Ease of Deployment: A containerized approach makes it easier to move between cloud environments.

Dockerfile Configuration

To containerize the CAP service, I created a Dockerfile:

 

# Base image
FROM node:20.18.0

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy package.json and package-lock.json to install dependencies
COPY gen/srv/package.json gen/srv/package-lock.json ./

# Install dependencies
RUN npm ci
COPY gen/srv /app

# Set environment variable for production
ENV NODE_ENV=production

# Expose the port your app runs on (adjust if necessary)
EXPOSE 4004

# Start the application
CMD ["npx", "cds", "serve"]

 

MTA File : Make sure the type is 'application'

Building and Deploying

Step 1: Build and Push Docker Image

First, build and push the image to a Docker registry:

 

docker build -t <your-docker-image>:latest .
docker push <your-docker-image>:latest

 

Step 2: Deploy the MTA Project

Using SAP's MTA deployment process:

 

 

mbt build
cf deploy <generated-mtar>.mtar

 

Build pack here is not nodejs

entry.title: Call for Speakers for SIT Melbourne
entry.content:
Hi SAP Melbourne Community,
Happy New Year 2025, hope everyone had some well needed rest and relaxation.
We had an awesome 2024 with your support and looking forward to your continued support in 2025.

This post is a call for speakers.

If you would like to practice your presentation skills in a relaxed, friendly and supportive atmosphere look no further. I presented at the previous one and it felt great.

Following are the planned dates for 2025
19th Feb
23rd May
20th Aug
19th Nov

It's not too late, if you would like to present on 19th Feb as well.

Cheers,
SAP Melbourne Community

entry.title: Implementation of Observability using OpenTelemetry in SAP BTP
entry.content:
Introduction

Observability plays a crucial role in distributed ecosystems, especially within SAP BTP, where various applications, integrations, workflows, and automations are developed. These components not only exchange data internally but also interact with numerous non-SAP microservices deployed within the customer landscape. In this demo, I highlight the key benefits of OpenTelemetry as a powerful framework for implementing observability in such a complex and distributed environment.

Observability demo using OpenTelemetry in SAP BTP 

SAP Business Technology Platform 

SAP Integration Suite 

SAP Cloud Application Programming Model

entry.title: using google chrome for automation in SAP BTP using CAP
entry.content:
Below are the steps to use google chrome in SAP BTP using SAP CAP. We will be using docker to install google chrome and use the build as docker instead of nodejs in MTA.YAML

Dockerfile

# Base image
FROM node:20.18.0

# Install required dependencies for Google Chrome and Puppeteer
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install gnupg wget -y && \
    wget --quiet --output-document=- https://dl-ssl.google.com/linux/linux_signing_key.pub | gpg --dearmor > /etc/apt/trusted.gpg.d/google-archive.gpg && \
    sh -c 'echo "deb [arch=amd64] http://dl.google.com/linux/chrome/deb/ stable main" >> /etc/apt/sources.list.d/google.list' && \
    apt-get update && \
    apt-get install google-chrome-stable -y --no-install-recommends && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy the current directory to /app in the Docker container
WORKDIR /app
# # Copy package.json and package-lock.json to install dependencies
COPY gen/srv/package.json gen/srv/package-lock.json ./

# Install dependencies
RUN npm ci
COPY gen/srv /app

# Set environment variable for production
ENV NODE_ENV=production

# # Expose the port your app runs on (adjust if necessary)
EXPOSE 4004

# # Start the application in watch mode
CMD ["npx", "cds", "serve"]

make sure build parameters no-source: true as this will ensure it uses docker for build. Once deployed you can use ssh to verify docker is installed

modules:
  - name: <app>-srv
    type: application
    parameters:
      docker:
        image: <docker-user-name>/<app>-dev:latest
      memory: 1G
      disk-quota: 3G
    requires:
      - name: <app>-uaa
    provides:
      - name: srv-api
        properties:
          srv-url: ${default-url} # Ensure this is set correctly
    build-parameters:
      no-source: true

entry.title: Enhancements for SAP Community - February 2025
entry.content:
Punxsutawney Phil saw his shadow on Sunday, so, there will be six more weeks of winter.  Here are some new features released this week and we hope that they will warm you up.

SAP Managed tag Picker

We love using tags, especially the managed ones.  They help make finding and referencing content much easier.  When creating a post, the SAP managed tag picker now supports the following features:

  • the search results are now sorted alphabetically - this will speed up the selection process
  • all results matching the search term are provided - nothing gets left behind!

        

 

 

 

 

 

 

Improvement to the SAP Managed tag page

The SAP Managed Tag page is a great way to view all content that references an SAP Managed tag.  These pages are always accessible by clicking on any linked manage tag.

 

 

In this new release, the layout of the SAP Managed tag page has been streamlined as follows:

  • Only the latest posts will be shown
  • Side widgets have been removed to horizontally extend the space for each post
  • A preview of the posts has been added 
  • Kudo counter has been added for each post

  

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

SAP Community and BlueSky

To coincide with the launch of the SAP Community's presence on BlueSky we have replaced the X icons and links with the BlueSky icon and link in notifications, SAP Community footer among other places across the community.

Stay tuned for more enhancements coming your way!

entry.title: EPAMCampus free SAP Development training program for beginners.
entry.content:
Elevate your career and master the art of creating powerful digital solutions for enterprises! Join the EPAMCampus SAP Development training program, designed specifically for beginners. This free training opportunity offers a methodical pathway to developing skills that are in high demand in today's market

To apply and launch an IT career journey: https://epa.ms/am-sap-lnk.

entry.title: SAP Tech Talks Community Events by EPAM
entry.content:
Hello SAP  Community in Yerevan!

I would like to invite you to join our Tech Talks community by EPAM. your SAP journey's perfect companion. Whether you're new to SAP or an experienced pro, we're your one-stop source for SAP insights.

What We Offer:

✅ Stay Informed: Get the latest IT news, trends, and expert insights.

✅ Engage: Connect with SAP enthusiasts and experts.

✅ SAP Events: Stay updated on SAP events and webinars.

✅ Best Practices: Learn from the best to excel in SAP technologies.

Join us to fuel innovation and proficiency in SAP technologies. Let's explore together!

I would like to bring your attention specifically to the latest event on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Pricing App designed to provide insights, strategies, and best practices for leveraging the SAP BTP to its full potential.

Whether you are looking to optimize your current SAP setup or considering adopting the SAP BTP, this webinar is a valuable opportunity to learn directly from the experts.

For further details and to register, please visit SAP BTP Pricing App Event. This is an opportunity you won't want to miss as you continue your SAP journey.

We look forward to seeing you there!

entry.title: New Perspectives From An Elder Community Member
entry.content:
In 2025, I volunteered to continue community service in the SAP "Ecosystem." Why, you might ask, as I have no access to any working SAP system other than the community. Part stubbornness, part "keep contributing", and part because many (but not all) of my global tech contacts started here. And in this world, we plan to get around.

A year ago, the SAP Community site was migrated from multiple prior systems to the current landscape. This monumental shift was preceded by announced plans to adjust content to the new design, testing, and user feedback. Some content was planned for removal for various reasons, which ended up less dramatic than anticipated, though some treasured moments are lost in time ("like tears in rain").

Time Settings

I chose to set the "time/date" display field to absolute dates as shown above, with a push-the-envelope value of "ddd" for the day of the month. Yes, months don't go above 99, so I get a leading 0 which tells me I am logged in since my settings work.

Good

All change, especially in web forms, brings challenges which people manage in a spectrum ranging from leading the wave, sitting in the middle, or trying to drag or object. The best result of the Community platform of having a coherent/cohesive set of controls was less visible to casual users who might not have recognized a WordPress blog post from a BlogSpot from a Medium. 

The history retention, keeping posts back to the early 2000's, enables those who might be gifted an old but running system to be migrated or archived, some clues, if they can set their search parameters correctly. I'm thinking the internal SAP site search will continue to improve over legacy external search sites that seem to bent on pushing quick fixes over useful results.

Other than the yet-to-be fixed bug with extraneous new-lines, I'm satisfied with the post editor for how I draft and share content. Yes, those familiar with the WordPress tool-kit are dismayed at the differences, to which I think of the maxim "it's a poor worker who blames their tool" for the results. I do miss the "you have 500 words here" feedback because one way I track my effort is number of words, which sort-of translates to reading comprehension time. I don't want to skimp on words, nor do I want to run on and one.

The rearrangement of the prior landscape to new spaces and categories is generally a success in my view, in particular separating posts by SAP staff from the rest of us hoi polloi. For this "year-in-review" post I chose the Welcome Corner with the idea that both new members and veterans will find this instructive.

Not-Quite-Good

In the hey-day of SCN, flurries of activities whirled around the recognition system, where members gained points for adding content, and could compare one group against another to demonstrate skills (real or imagined) in solving roll-out, ramp-up, or cool-down obstacles. "Cheaters" were dealt with via moderation, and "Rock Stars" were given platforms to continue contributing. The new platform has potential for both awards and consequences, and both sides need care and feeding. A common error is asking a question as a part of a prior thread (which is also common on other Q&A sites); some of our attempts to correct this behavior have worked, some not. Badges and contests have slowed to a trickle in 2025.

The changes from having blog post authority to needing approval in certain spaces was inevitable, I presume, but has caused some to move to other places or stop contributing. I've had delays and other obstacles that might have driven off someone less motivated to contribute. The mothballing ("sunset") of the Information Architecture space was poorly communicated and executed, in my opinion. I can understand if an entity does a reorg, or adds/removes products from the portfolio. But I had a draft ready to publish when I learned that area was being removed. Or maybe being set read-only. Or, what.

Going to a Universal ID was another change that somewhat coincided with the platform change, and for most members was not a big deal. For others, though, losing access or privileges was a diversion from contributing which might result in simply giving up. Over a year later, I still run into inexplicable "Authentication Unsuccessful" messages and unpredictable timeouts.

Needs Work

Among the faults in the current platform is the lack of a central content view per member. If you only stay in one topic space that's not an issue but is you stray into multiple areas you may be unable to find a draft you started then didn't finish or submit for approval. In this space ("Welcome Corner Blog Posts"), I could see the draft of this content, but I also see 2 other alleged drafts.

Drafts + 2 not-drafts

The content from 2018 is not really a draft despite the title, and I would likely never have noticed until I started new content (the assignment of prior content to current spaces was done by an algorithm).

There seem to be undeveloped areas of recognition and identity, unsurprising given the constant rise-and-fall of social media sites one might wish to share. Adding a custom footer with your signature is nice, and cool [see https://community.sap.com/t5/what-s-new/new-sap-community-profile-signatures/ba-p/13598678] but does seem like a clunky afterthought.

Lastly, the prevalence of spam posts over the past year has been a battle. Because the site needs to allow new users to join, there seemed no way to prevent infiltration of scummy content like money-services. As they targeted larger groups, the noise became unbearable, causing me to un-subscribe from notifications. Which then caused me to think less about posting here (out of sight, out of mind). Kudos to those in the Community office repelling the boarders and keeping the content from being overwhelmed with irrelevancies or worse.

Final deep thoughts

I have not mothballed the ABAP Detective series quite yet. Keep circulating the tapes.

entry.title: SITVIE 2025 - Schedule is now online
entry.content:
We are excited to announce that the schedule for SAP Inside Track 2025 is now available!

Join us for a day of interesting talks and network with your local SAP community: get your ticket here

TimeSession
09:00Opening
(SITVIE Orga Team)
09:15Embedded Analytics - underestimated and ignored
(Sebastian Nigl)
09:40Clean Core Extensibility:
What is in it? How to measure? How clean can one be?
(Lukas Bretschneider)
10:25Deploy and Manage SAP BTP with Terraform
(Akos Farkas)
11:05Break
11:20Say Hello! to ADT IDE Actions
A new and simple framework for extending the ADT with your own ideas
(Thomas Alexander Ritter)
12:05Clean SQLScript - write code that your colleagues don't hate you for
(Jörg Brandeis)
12:45Lunch
13:45The importance of soft skills and psychological knowledge for SAP consultants
(Markus Waldl)
14:30WYSIWYG and label print in SAP
(Regina Hochecker / Oliver Wahrstötter)
14:55What's new in ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model
(Andre Fischer)
15:35Break
16:05Event Driven Integration
(Martin Muttenthaler)
16:50Unleash Beyond Boundaries:
Building Generative AI Scenarios with the ABAP AI SDK
(Ingo Bräuninger)
17:30Closing
(SITVIE Orga Team)


Interested in sponsoring? We'd love to hear from you. 🙂
See https://sit.wien/become-a-sponsor for details.


See you soon,
SITVIE team

entry.title: New Jersey SAP Community 1st Meet in 2025
entry.content:
🌟What an incredible experience at the First SAP Community Meet in 2025 New Jersey! 🌟

The event was a fantastic opportunity to meet some of the brightest minds in SAP consulting. From deep discussions on the latest SAP innovations to personal stories about overcoming challenges, it was inspiring to see such a passionate community come together.

Key Takeaways:

  • Networking: I met so many amazing SAP consultants from different industries. It’s great to know that we’re all in this journey together!
  • Innovations: The presentations on SAP S/4HANA and AI-driven business solutions were eye-opening. It’s clear that the future of SAP is full of exciting possibilities.
  • Collaboration: The roundtable discussions were invaluable, as they gave us the chance to exchange ideas and gain new perspectives from peers.

🚀I’m looking forward to attending more of these events and continuing to learn and grow with the SAP community.

 

Resource NameExpertiseContribution in Meet
Rajasekhar NethiRajasekhar Nethi possesses extensive experience across various SAP modules, showcasing a deep understanding of SAP's core functionalities and its evolving capabilities.During the session, he provided valuable insights into the latest trends in SAP, addressing advancements in technology and innovations within the SAP ecosystem. This included emerging solutions and updates that are shaping the future of SAP implementations.
Suresh SadhuSuresh Sadhu has been actively engaged in SAP Community discussions, contributed extensive knowledge and practical experiences related to SAP technologies (FICO, CFIN, FSCM).Organized and coordinated all the members to attend the meeting, making it successful. 
Sitaram ThotaSitaram Thota brings significant experience in migration of legacy SAP systems to S/4HANA environment along with selective configuration & data migration, specializing in the seamless transition of data from legacy systems to SAP environments.He shared his extensive knowledge on the intricacies of system conversion, data migration, covering key aspects such as data extraction, transformation, loading (ETL), and the critical importance of data quality and integrity throughout the migration process.
Krishna ChaitanyaKrishna Chaitanya delivered a comprehensive presentation on the integration of SAP with Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML), demonstrating their transformative potential in modern business landscapes.He offered a detailed analysis of real-world business scenarios where AI and ML are effectively applied within SAP systems. The session highlighted how these technologies can drive automation, optimize decision-making, and enhance business processes, further extending the capabilities of SAP solutions.
Phanindra Phanindra Reddy Sayala is an SAP S/4HANA PP & QM consultant specializing in digital manufacturing, IoT, and data science. He has led MES-SAP integrations, enabling real-time shop floor automation and predictive maintenance using AI.Phanindra has explained about Digital twin concept.
Scenario: A manufacturing plant has a complex assembly line with numerous machines.
Digital Twin Implementation: Sensors are installed on each machine to monitor key performance indicators like temperature, vibration, and pressure. This data is fed into a digital twin of the assembly line. 
LohithSAP FICO Techno-functional consultant with good knowledge in streamlining Pharmacy Benefit Management (SAP-PBM) solutions by integrating claims processing, formulary control, and cost optimization, while providing real-time analytics and ensuring regulatory compliance.Lohith contributed his ideas by sharing the challenges that current industries are facing and the solutions provided by SAP to enhance operational efficiency, reduce drug costs, and shared insights on how we can utilize SAP’s reporting tools to gain better understanding of medication usage, cost trends, and patient outcomes for improved decision-making.
Vishnu Vardhan MVishnu Vardhan M has over 8 years of experience as an SAP PP & QM consultant. He has played a key role in multiple SAP PP implementations, optimizing manufacturing processes. His contributions to the industry include expertise in automation, digital transformation, and sustainable manufacturing solutions.He provided a detailed analysis of how machines will handle repetitive tasks, freeing humans to focus on creativity and decision-making. By leveraging AI and robotics, manufacturing will evolve toward large-scale customization, enabling the production of personalized products. Additionally, AI will enhance sustainability by optimizing processes to minimize waste and energy consumption. Advanced technologies like Cobos, 3D printing, and AI will improve manufacturing speed, efficiency, and quality. This transformative innovation will pave the way for a smarter, more sustainable, and highly personalized future of production.

entry.title: WalkMe – More than a walk through
entry.content:
It's common knowledge by now that the Concur User Assistant (CUA) is powered by WalkMe and that there are two versions of the CUA - Standard and Custom

Standard content that has been pre-built by SAP Concur that your users can access via Explore Interactive Help from the Help menu or other on-screen prompts.

 

 

Custom built content which has been built specifically for your entity either internally, through a Partner, or with an SAP Concur project team.

A lot of the time it can be viewed that the CUA only provides Smart Walk Throughs (SWT) where users are guided through a particular process through step-by-step on-screen prompts, or with Smart Tips where users can hover over an icon and can view further details.

WalkMe has more potential than this. A lot more.

 

The example that I am going to focus on in this article is one that has come up on a few projects - Users entering credit card expenses as cash expenses.

You know the scenario - a user may not always have the patience to wait for the credit card provider to process the expense, for it to be sent to Concur and then released to the user to add to a claim/report (*). This can lead to a reconciliation irregularity and the need for the accounts/finance team to perform reversals etc. to bring everything back into line.

 (*) I use UK English, where an Expense Report is more commonly known as an Expense Claim

I'll take you through the steps on how you can modify the user’s behavior when building your own custom content.

Step 1

Create a Launcher that will sit on top of the Add Expense button inside a report.

 

 

This will end up becoming an invisible Launcher, so that users will not actually realize they are clicking on it to begin with. But, for the purpose of building and testing it is best left visible so we can see what we are doing with it.

 

Step 2

Create an Automatic Smart Walk Through that will always take the user to the "Available Expenses" tab of the Add Expense to Report popup. What can happen is when a user clicks on the Add Expense button, the pop-up defaults to the last used setting, which can sometimes be the "New Expense" tab; Users then just go on to create the new expense rather than taking a look at what they have available.

 

The aim here will be to mimic Concur's normal behavior - the SWT will need to have two Auto-Steps

  1. Clicking the "Add Expense" button on behalf of the user.
  2. Clicking on the "Available Expenses" tab

 

Step 3

Connect the newly created SWT to the Launcher

 The overall flow will look like this:

User clicks on invisible launcher -> Launcher triggers SWT -> SWT clicks on Add Expense -> SWT clicks on Available Expenses

 

Step 4

Once you are happy with the testing, you can then make the Launcher invisible by selecting Edit on Page:

 

And then selecting the ghost icon:

Don’t forget to Save!

You can, of course, take it a further as the above method doesn't stop uses navigating to the New Expense tab. This is where you can in components to actively warn users against making cash expense entries where they should use what is in their Available Expenses.

Step 5

Here you can employ another invisible Launcher on the New Expense tab.

As before, it remains visible so you can see it for testing and building out the solution.

 

Step 6

This Launcher will kick off another Smart Walk Through - This time however you can start with a popup for the user to warn them.

 

 

Step 7

Add an auto-step to click on the New Expense tab for the users who are making a genuine cash expense entry.

 

As before, once you are happy, don't forget to make the New Expenses Launcher invisible!

 

Every time a user then clicks on, what they believe to be the New Expense tab, it will kick off this SWT and they will receive the warning that you have written.

Of course, the above is just an example - the wording that you can use is very much up to you.

 

Overall, this is just a small sample of what you can achieve with a customized WalkMe solution.
